summary: Applied ADR 0226 on proxmox-host from the rebased branch head by installing the repo-managed host control-loop systemd service, timer, and path units, repairing a oneshot start-limit lockout discovered during live verification, and re-verifying scheduled/manual plus path-triggered runs end to end on the live Proxmox host.

verification.check: Focused workflow contract and repo validation slice
verification.observed: `make workflow-info WORKFLOW=configure-host-control-loops`, `uv run --with pytest pytest -q tests/test_proxmox_host_control_loops_role.py`, and `./scripts/validate_repo.sh workstream-surfaces agent-standards yaml json role-argument-specs ansible-syntax data-models` all passed from rebased branch head `b56e6dee15d4caca0e326235bdc7403a4b426e49`.

`make configure-host-control-loops` completed successfully from `b56e6dee15d4caca0e326235bdc7403a4b426e49` with `proxmox-host ok=33 changed=2 failed=0`; the replay updated the service unit and cleared stale failed state for `lv3-host-control-loop-reconcile.service` before reasserting the timer and path units.

`systemctl show` on `proxmox-host` reported `lv3-host-control-loop-reconcile.service Result=success ActiveState=inactive`, the timer as `active/waiting` with `NextElapseUSecRealtime=Sat 2026-03-28 18:00:59 CET` and `LastTriggerUSec=Sat 2026-03-28 17:31:04 CET`, and the path unit as `active/waiting` with `Triggers=lv3-host-control-loop-reconcile.service`.

`sudo systemctl start lv3-host-control-loop-reconcile.service` recorded `trigger: scheduled_or_manual` at `2026-03-28T16:39:33Z` with `history_path: /var/lib/lv3-host-control-loops/runs/20260328T163933Z.json`, and the journal showed a clean start and finish at `17:39:33 CET`.

Writing request `ws-0226-path-20260328T163913Z` to `/var/lib/lv3-host-control-loops/requests/reconcile.request` was consumed immediately; `latest.json` recorded `trigger: path_request` with the request payload at `2026-03-28T16:39:13Z`, the journal showed a clean start and finish at `17:39:13 CET`, and the history file `/var/lib/lv3-host-control-loops/runs/20260328T163913Z.json` was created.

`make pre-push-gate` was exercised on this branch and failed only on shared integration surfaces outside the ADR 0226 ownership set: `docs/diagrams` is stale for `agent-coordination-map.excalidraw`, `build/platform-manifest.json` is out of date, and the containerized `ansible-lint` step timed out on the local arm64 host while using the amd64 check-runner image. The ADR 0226-specific validation slice above remained green.

notes: The first rebased live verification exposed a contract bug in the original oneshot service definition: `Restart=on-failure` plus `StartLimitIntervalSec=900` and `StartLimitBurst=3` can trip `start-limit-hit` after normal timer, path, and manual starts because systemd rate-limits successful starts too.

The repaired branch head delegates retry to the timer, path trigger, or explicit operator start and adds a safe `systemctl reset-failed` step during replay so a latched failed state is cleared automatically on the next apply.

Protected integration files intentionally remain untouched on this workstream branch; merge-to-main still needs the repo version bump, changelog and README updates, `versions/stack.yaml` receipt mapping, and any shared generated-artifact refresh required by the current mainline gate.

correction_loop.repair_action: Removed the service-level restart and start-limit settings for the oneshot unit, added `systemctl reset-failed` to the replay path, and reapplied the role from `b56e6dee15d4caca0e326235bdc7403a4b426e49`.
correction_loop.verification: The rebased replay completed with `ok=33 changed=2 failed=0`, manual `systemctl start` now records `trigger: scheduled_or_manual`, and the path-triggered request `ws-0226-path-20260328T163913Z` is consumed successfully without returning to `start-limit-hit`.
